titles.title: TarGene: A Nextflow pipeline for the estimation of genetic effects on human traits via semi-parametric methods.

citation.article_title: Computationally efficient whole-genome regression for quantitative and binary traits
citation.author: Mbatchou
citation.unstructured_citation: Mbatchou, J., Barnard, L., Backman, J., Marcketta, A., Kosmicki, J. A., Ziyatdinov, A., Benner, C., O’Dushlaine, C., Barber, M., Boutkov, B., & others. (2021). Computationally efficient whole-genome regression for quantitative and binary traits. Nature Genetics, 53(7), 1097–1103. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41588-021-00870-7

citation.article_title: DeepNull models non-linear covariate effects to improve phenotypic prediction and association power
citation.author: McCaw
citation.unstructured_citation: McCaw, Z. R., Colthurst, T., Yun, T., Furlotte, N. A., Carroll, A., Alipanahi, B., McLean, C. Y., & Hormozdiari, F. (2022). DeepNull models non-linear covariate effects to improve phenotypic prediction and association power. Nature Communications, 13(1), 241. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-021-27930-0

citation.article_title: False discovery rate control in genome-wide association studies with population structure
citation.author: Sesia
citation.journal_title: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
citation.unstructured_citation: Sesia, M., Bates, S., Candès, E., Marchini, J., & Sabatti, C. (2021). False discovery rate control in genome-wide association studies with population structure. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, 118(40), e2105841118. https://doi.org/10.1073/pnas.2105841118

citation.author: Di Tommaso

citation.article_title: PLINK: A tool set for whole-genome association and population-based linkage analyses
citation.author: Purcell
citation.journal_title: The American journal of human genetics

PLINK: A tool set for whole-genome association and population-based linkage analyses. The American Journal of Human Genetics, 81(3), 559–575. https://doi.org/10.1086/519795

citation.article_title: Efficiently controlling for case-control imbalance and sample relatedness in large-scale genetic association studies

citation.unstructured_citation: Zhou, W., Nielsen, J. B., Fritsche, L. G., Dey, R., Gabrielsen, M. E., Wolford, B. N., LeFaive, J., VandeHaar, P., Gagliano, S. A., Gifford, A., & others. (2018). Efficiently controlling for case-control imbalance and sample relatedness in large-scale genetic association studies. Nature Genetics, 50(9), 1335–1341. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41588-018-0184-y

citation.article_title: Semiparametric efficient estimation of small genetic effects in large-scale population cohorts
citation.author: Labayle
citation.unstructured_citation: Labayle, O., Roskams-Hieter, B., Slaughter, J., Tetley-Campbell, K., Laan, M. J. van der, Ponting, C. P., Beentjes, S. V., & Khamseh, A. (2025). Semiparametric efficient estimation of small genetic effects in large-scale population cohorts. Biostatistics, 26(1), kxaf030. https://doi.org/10.1093/biostatistics/kxaf030
